headline: A US export-control directive forced Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all customers within three days of release, dominating the month.
body: The Fable 5 and Mythos 5 shutdown drove a wave of model-regulation, export-control, and geopolitical-restriction coverage, with reports citing jailbreak concerns and questions about whether frontier models stay public or move to government-only access [2][3][5][7][8]. In parallel, inference throughput broke out: Xiaomi and TileRT's trillion-parameter MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed and Google's DiffusionGemma both cleared 1,000 tokens per second, the latter by shifting text generation to a diffusion approach that moves inference from memory-bound to compute-bound [1][6][9]. On the technique side, memory systems and KV cache quantization broke out alongside new entrants Model Context Protocol, JSON-RPC, client-server communication, and state persistence. Vibe coding and browser automation led the tooling rotation, with Google Antigravity surfacing agent-first development across desktop, IDE, CLI, and SDK and the AIventure project teaching agentic Gemma-powered workflows [10][11].
